decisions.description: 4-phase implementation approach (A: Token Capture, B: Multi-Account Refresh, C: Multi-Account UI, D: Polish)
decisions.rationale: Decomposition provides clear dependency ordering and incremental risk reduction

decisions.description: OQ-3 resolution: exclude stale accounts from worst-case menubar calculation
decisions.rationale: Resolves ADR ambiguity; prevents edge-case worst-case inflation from dormant accounts

decisions.description: B-pre task added: fix recursive retry defer bug before Phase B
decisions.rationale: Risk analyst identified existing bug that would compound during multi-account refresh implementation

decisions.description: C12 task added: move stale account removal from Phase D to Phase C
decisions.rationale: Writer synthesis decision based on dependency ordering; prevents late-stage integration issues

riskContext.criticalFinding: CRIT-1: Design spec Interaction Patterns contradicts OQ-3 resolution on stale account inclusion

notes.contextContinuation: Session continued from prior context (context limit). Phase 1 outputs reconstructed from conversation summary. Despite context loss, Plan Writer produced comprehensive plan because source artifacts (ADR, design spec) were on disk.
notes.readinessAssessment: Plan ready for Phase A implementation. Three revisions required before Phase C: (1) correct design spec, (2) cover fallback keychain path, (3) add D-pre task for XCTest setup.
